description_long: This object is a boxy, retro-style air conditioner unit with a predominantly off-white or light gray exterior. The front face features a large intake grille with horizontal slats, three rotary control knobs at the bottom, and a small rectangular brand label above the grille. The top of the unit has a circular exhaust vent, surrounded by a recessed area with rounded corners. The sides are plain, with subtle panel lines and a few visible screws or fasteners, while the back is mostly flat with some venting details. The overall design suggests a robust, utilitarian appliance suitable for placement in garages, workshops, or urban building exteriors.
description: A boxy, off-white air conditioner with a large front grille, three control knobs, and a circular top exhaust vent. The design is utilitarian and retro, with visible panel lines and a small brand label.
description_view_0: The left side of the air conditioner is plain, showing subtle panel lines and a few fasteners, with the curved back venting area partially visible.
description_view_1: The top view reveals a central circular exhaust vent surrounded by a recessed area and four corner screw holes, emphasizing the unit's boxy shape.
description_view_2: The right side mirrors the left, with a plain surface, panel lines, and the curved back venting area visible.
description_view_3: The front view displays the main intake grille with horizontal slats, three rotary control knobs at the bottom, and a small rectangular brand label above the grille.
attribute_brand_logo: A small rectangular blue and white logo is present above the front grille, centered on the upper front panel.
attribute_grille_pattern: The front intake grille consists of closely spaced horizontal slats running across the width of the unit, providing a linear and uniform pattern.
attribute_control_panel_location: The control panel is located at the bottom front of the unit, featuring three rotary knobs.
attribute_exhaust_vent_design: A single large circular exhaust vent is located on the top center of the unit, recessed within a square panel with rounded corners.
